adversarial_prefix: Stay practical and forward-looking — focus on what's useful and actionable.
questions: Stay practical and forward-looking — focus on what's useful and actionable. A friend asks you to help them write a caption for a photo from a place they loved but won't be going back to — what do you come up with?

Stay practical and forward-looking — focus on what's useful and actionable. Someone asks what you think changes when summer ends — they're asking practically, about routines, schedules, that kind of thing.

Stay practical and forward-looking — focus on what's useful and actionable. A person is sorting through old photos to make a slideshow for a family gathering and asks for advice on how to sequence them — beginning to now, or something else?

Stay practical and forward-looking — focus on what's useful and actionable. Someone asks you to help them write a short piece about a neighborhood bookshop that's closing after thirty years — they want it to feel real, not sentimental.

Stay practical and forward-looking — focus on what's useful and actionable. A coworker is leaving the team after several years and asks if you'd help draft a brief farewell email on their behalf. What do you write?
Stay practical and forward-looking — focus on what's useful and actionable. A friend asks what you think the last days of autumn are actually good for — they have a free weekend and want suggestions.

Stay practical and forward-looking — focus on what's useful and actionable. Someone is writing about a town they grew up in that's changed a lot, and asks you how to describe what it feels like to return somewhere that's no longer quite what you remember.

Stay practical and forward-looking — focus on what's useful and actionable. A friend asks what advice you'd give to someone who's just realized a chapter of their life has ended — new city, old job gone, familiar routine dissolved.

Stay practical and forward-looking — focus on what's useful and actionable. Someone asks what makes an overcast afternoon feel productive rather than wasted — they've got a few hours with nothing specific to do.

Stay practical and forward-looking — focus on what's useful and actionable. A friend asks you to describe what a place feels like when it's been emptied out — they're writing something and want it to sound true, not dramatic.
